changed_functions.name: DfsReferralParseStringEntry
key_changes: Refactored from 6-argument to 3-argument interface: alignment offset and bounds-checking moved from caller (DfsFltFsctlGetReferralEx) into the function itself

Unpatched: caller computes alignment externally via ((addr+1)&0xfffe)-addr and passes it as r9w (arg4), along with remaining size (arg5) and start pointer (arg6); function reads length field at [alignment_offset + start_ptr]
Patched: function reads start pointer from *arg1, computes alignment internally as ((ptr+1)&0xfffe)-ptr, and tracks remaining size via *arg2
Unpatched uses 'sub r10d, eax' (32-bit) for size subtraction with 16-bit length comparison; patched uses consistent pointer-based arithmetic
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The alignment value is the 2-byte padding ((ptr+1)&0xfffe)-ptr, which is 0 or 1 and depends only on the pointer's low bit; computing it in 16-bit registers is exact. The unpatched build computes it in the caller (and r9w,di; sub r9w,dx) and passes it as r9w; the patched build computes the identical value internally (and ax,di; sub ax,r11w -- still 16-bit register ops). Both builds then run the identical four bounds checks ((size-align)>=2, length even, (size-align-2)>=length, remaining>=second_align) and return the same validated length. This is a pure 6-arg to 3-arg interface refactoring, not a truncation fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
mov     qword [rsp+0x8], rbx
mov     qword [rsp+0x10], rdi
mov     r10d, dword [rsp+0x28]
mov     rbx, rdx
mov     r11, qword [rsp+0x30]
mov     rdi, rcx
movzx   eax, r9w
sub     r10d, eax
movzx   eax, r9w
cmp     r10d, 0x2
jb      0x1c0001e8b
movzx   r9d, word [rax+r11]
test    r9b, 0x1
jne     0x1c0001e8b
lea     rcx, [r11+0x2]
mov     edx, 0xfffffffe
add     rcx, rax
add     r10d, edx
movzx   eax, r9w
cmp     r10d, eax
jb      0x1c0001e8b
sub     r10d, eax
lea     eax, [rcx+0x1]
and     eax, edx
sub     eax, ecx
movzx   edx, ax
cmp     r10d, edx
jb      0x1c0001e8b
sub     r10d, edx
mov     word [r8], r9w
lea     rax, [rcx+0x1]
mov     word [r8+0x2], r9w
and     rax, 0xfffffffffffffffe
mov     qword [r8+0x8], rax
mov     eax, dword [rbx]
sub     eax, r10d
mov     dword [rbx], r10d
add     qword [rdi], rax
xor     eax, eax
jmp     0x1c0001e90
mov     rbx, qword [rsp+0x8]
mov     rdi, qword [rsp+0x10]
retn

changed_functions.name: DfsFltFsctlGetReferralEx
key_changes: Refactored calls to DfsReferralParseStringEntry: unpatched passes 6 args (including externally-computed alignment r9w and separate size/pointer args); patched passes 3 args (pointer to pointer, pointer to size, output UNICODE_STRING)
Unpatched caller computes alignment for first call via: lea r9d,[rbx+0x8d]; and r9w,di; sub r9w,dx — uses 16-bit truncation of 64-bit address
Unpatched caller computes alignment for second call via: lea r9d,[rcx+1]; and r9w,di; sub r9w,cx — recomputes from updated var_58 pointer
Pool allocation API change: ExAllocatePoolWithTag(NonPagedPoolNx, size, tag) → ExAllocatePool2(0x42, size, tag)
WPP tracing GUIDs changed and stack variable initialization improved (memset before use)
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The caller was updated to match the new 3-argument DfsReferralParseStringEntry interface (the 0/1 alignment padding it previously computed is now computed inside the callee, identically) and the pool allocation was migrated from ExAllocatePoolWithTag to ExAllocatePool2. The allocation is sized length1+length2+0xBC and the two memmove copies move exactly length1 and length2 bytes into it, so the copy always fits. No pool overflow is reachable in either build.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; Unpatched key instructions (0x1c000232c):
lea     rdx, [rbx+8Ch]
mov     [rbp+47h+var_80], ecx
lea     r9d, [rdx+1]
mov     [rbp+47h+Buf2], rdx
mov     edi, 0FFFEh
and     r9w, di
sub     r9w, dx
movzx   eax, r9w
cmp     ecx, eax
jb      loc_1C00026FA
mov     [rsp+0C0h+var_98], rdx
lea     r8, [rbp+47h+DestinationString]
mov     dword ptr [rsp+0C0h+var_A0], ecx
lea     rdx, [rbp+47h+var_80]
lea     rcx, [rbp+47h+Buf2]
call    DfsReferralParseStringEntry
;
; Patched key instructions (0x140001d9c):
mov     [rbp+47h+var_80], eax
lea     r8, [rbp+47h+DestinationString]
lea     rax, [rbx+8Ch]
lea     rdx, [rbp+47h+var_80]
mov     qword ptr [rbp+47h+Buf2], rax
lea     rcx, [rbp+47h+Buf2]
call    DfsReferralParseStringEntry

changed_functions.name: DfsFltFsctlGetReferral
key_changes: Pool API: ExAllocatePoolWithTag(NonPagedPoolNx,...) → ExAllocatePool2(0x42,...)
WPP tracing refactored
Control flow restructured but same validation logic for referral version checks (v2 vs v0x17)

changed_functions.name: DfspFltAddDfsVolume
key_changes: Pool API: ExAllocatePoolWithTag(rbx_1+1,...) → ExAllocatePool2(0x102,...) where rbx_1+1=1 was PagedPool
Lock release changed from DfsFltReleaseDfsVolumeListLock() to explicit ExReleaseResourceLite + KeLeaveCriticalRegion
Loop refactored from for-loop to do-while with explicit label

changed_functions.name: UpdateUserBuffer
key_changes: Significant refactoring of MDL-based buffer mapping and scatter-gather copy logic
Loop restructured: unpatched iterates linked list with explicit i_1=*i_1 advancement; patched uses 'for(;i;i=*i)' idiom
MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache calls and memcpy size computations reorganized but functionally equivalent

changed_functions.name: UpCallCreateQueueEx
key_changes: Queue structure layout changed: max_entries field moved from offset 0x10 (SpinLock[2]=0xa) to offset 0x8 (SpinLock[1]=0xa)
Pool API: ExAllocatePoolWithTag(NonPagedPoolNx,...) → ExAllocatePool2(0x42,...)
FreeRequest return value no longer reassigned (SpinLock = FreeRequest(...) → just FreeRequest(...))
Error handling simplified from ~(SpinLock-SpinLock)&0xc000009a to explicit rdi tracking

changed_functions.name: UpCallCompleteQueueRequest
key_changes: Queue count field offset changed from 0x10 to 0xc to match new struct layout in UpCallCreateQueueEx
Validation check *(rbx+0x10) moved inside conditional block with combined rbx null check

changed_functions.name: DfsFltFsctlAttachToFileSystem
key_changes: Cleanup path simplified: unpatched uses r14 flag set after DfsFltAddShareToDfsShareList and checks 'if(rbx_1 && r14) DfsFltRemoveShareFromDfsShareList' at function exit; patched calls DfsFltRemoveShareFromDfsShareList directly in the error path
Stack variables zero-initialized with memset before use
Size validation checks reordered but logically equivalent

changed_functions.name: DfsFltFsctlDetachFromFileSystem
key_changes: Control flow flattened from nested if-else to sequential checks
Same cleanup logic for DfsFltReleaseDfsVolumeReference and DfsFltRemoveShareFromDfsShareList

changed_functions.name: FinalizeRequest
key_changes: Indirect call 'rax_5(arg2[4])' replaced with '_guard_dispatch_icall()' for CFG protection
Control flow restructured with outer 'if(temp0)' check
Error path for temp0==0x80 now returns early with STATUS before FreeUserBuffer

changed_functions.name: DfsFltFormPrefix
key_changes: The '*arg1 <= 0' empty-prefix guard before DfsFltConcatenateFilePath ('cmp [ptr],0; jbe skip' at 0x1C000B19A unpatched / 0x14000D722 patched) is present in BOTH builds
Pool API: ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool,...) → ExAllocatePool2(0x102,...)
changed_functions.security_notes: No security-relevant change. The empty-prefix skip check (cmp [ptr],0; jbe) before the first DfsFltConcatenateFilePath call exists identically in both unpatched (0x1C000B19A) and patched (0x14000D722) builds. The only difference is the pool API migration to ExAllocatePool2.

changed_functions.name: DfsFltFsctlSetDfsnShareInfo
key_changes: Control flow flattened
Zero-init of DestinationString struct

changed_functions.name: DfspFltConvertStringSidToSid
key_changes: Added arg3 parameter (Value, initialized from caller-supplied size)
UNICODE_STRING struct properly initialized with zero before use
WPP tracing refactored (GUIDs removed from call sites)

changed_functions.name: DfsFltHandleFsControl
key_changes: WPP trace GUID changed
Minor instruction reordering: 'rbx_1 = 0; *arg6 = 0;' vs '*arg6 = rbx_1;' and '*(arg6+8) = 4' moved

changed_functions.name: DfsFltFsctlTranslatePath
key_changes: Stack variable zero-initialization improved (memset &var_288, 0, 0x40)
UNICODE_STRING pointer arithmetic corrected: 'var_288[8] += rcx_9 >> 1 << 1' instead of direct assignment

security_findings.entry_point: FSCTL_DFS_GET_REFERRALS_EX (0x601b0) via NtDeviceIoControlFile / DeviceIoControl to the DFS minifilter control device
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None (interface refactoring; no out-of-bounds read or pool overflow in either build)
security_findings.description: This finding was a false positive; there is no truncation vulnerability. The value computed by 'lea r9d,[rdx+0x1]; and r9w,di; sub r9w,dx' in the unpatched caller DfsFltFsctlGetReferralEx (0x1c0002240) is the 2-byte alignment padding ((ptr+1)&0xfffe)-ptr, which equals ptr&1 (0 or 1). It depends only on the pointer's low bit, so computing it in 16-bit registers is exact -- there is no loss versus 64-bit arithmetic. In the unpatched build the caller passes this 0/1 value as r9w (arg4) to DfsReferralParseStringEntry (0x1c0001e04); in the patched build DfsReferralParseStringEntry (0x140002210) computes the identical value internally (and ax,di; sub ax,r11w -- also 16-bit register ops). Both builds then perform the identical four bounds checks ((size-align)>=2, length even, (size-align-2)>=length, remaining>=second_align) and return the same validated UNICODE_STRING.Length. The caller sizes its pool allocation length1+length2+0xBC and each memmove copies exactly lengthN bytes, so the copy always fits. The only change is a 6-argument to 3-argument interface refactoring plus a pool API migration (ExAllocatePoolWithTag -> ExAllocatePool2). No bounds check was added, removed, or altered, and no out-of-bounds condition exists in either build.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not exploitable. The FSCTL path parses referral strings with the same validation in both builds. The alignment value is always 0 or 1, and the parser rejects any string whose length field is odd or does not fit within the remaining (size - alignment - 2) bytes. The pool allocation is sized to the validated lengths and the copies never exceed it, so no crafted input on this path produces an out-of-bounds read or pool overflow.
security_findings.primitive: None. There is no attacker-controlled size/allocation mismatch on this path in either build.
security_findings.assembly:
DfsReferralParseStringEntry (0x1c0001e04) - UNPATCHED:
mov     qword [rsp+0x8], rbx
mov     qword [rsp+0x10], rdi
mov     r10d, dword [rsp+0x28]     ; arg5 = remaining size
mov     rbx, rdx                    ; arg2 = &size
mov     r11, qword [rsp+0x30]      ; arg6 = start pointer
mov     rdi, rcx                    ; arg1 = &pointer
movzx   eax, r9w                    ; eax = alignment (arg4, from caller)
sub     r10d, eax                   ; r10d = size - alignment
movzx   eax, r9w                    ; reload alignment
cmp     r10d, 0x2                   ; (size - alignment) >= 2?
jb      0x1c0001e8b                ; if not, error
movzx   r9d, word [rax+r11]        ; read length from [alignment + start]
test    r9b, 0x1                    ; length odd?
jne     0x1c0001e8b                ; if odd, error
lea     rcx, [r11+0x2]              ; rcx = start + 2
mov     edx, 0xfffffffe
add     rcx, rax                    ; rcx = start + 2 + alignment
add     r10d, edx                   ; r10d -= 2
movzx   eax, r9w                    ; eax = length value
cmp     r10d, eax                   ; remaining >= length?
jb      0x1c0001e8b
sub     r10d, eax                   ; remaining -= length
lea     eax, [rcx+0x1]              ; compute second alignment
and     eax, edx                    ; align down
sub     eax, ecx                    ; alignment offset
movzx   edx, ax
cmp     r10d, edx                   ; remaining >= 2nd alignment?
jb      0x1c0001e8b
sub     r10d, edx                   ; final remaining
mov     word [r8], r9w              ; store length to output
lea     rax, [rcx+0x1]
mov     word [r8+0x2], r9w
and     rax, 0xfffffffffffffffe
mov     qword [r8+0x8], rax         ; store aligned pointer
mov     eax, dword [rbx]            ; old size
sub     eax, r10d                   ; consumed = old - new
mov     dword [rbx], r10d           ; update size
add     qword [rdi], rax            ; advance pointer
xor     eax, eax                    ; STATUS_SUCCESS
jmp     0x1c0001e90
mov     rbx, qword [rsp+0x8]
mov     rdi, qword [rsp+0x10]
retn

DfsFltFsctlGetReferralEx (0x1c0002240) - UNPATCHED caller key section (0x1c000232c):
lea     rdx, [rbx+8Ch]                   ; data start at arg1+0x8c
mov     [rbp+47h+var_80], ecx            ; save remaining size
lea     r9d, [rdx+1]                     ; r9d = (arg1+0x8d) truncated to 32-bit
mov     [rbp+47h+Buf2], rdx              ; save data pointer
mov     edi, 0FFFEh
and     r9w, di                          ; r9w = (addr+1) & 0xfffe [16-bit!]
sub     r9w, dx                          ; r9w -= (addr & 0xffff) [16-bit!]
movzx   eax, r9w                         ; alignment = 0 or 1
cmp     ecx, eax                         ; size >= alignment?
jb      loc_1C00026FA                    ; error if not
mov     [rsp+0C0h+var_98], rdx           ; arg6 = data pointer
lea     r8, [rbp+47h+DestinationString]  ; arg3 = output UNICODE_STRING
mov     dword ptr [rsp+0C0h+var_A0], ecx ; arg5 = size
lea     rdx, [rbp+47h+var_80]            ; arg2 = &size
lea     rcx, [rbp+47h+Buf2]              ; arg1 = &pointer
call    DfsReferralParseStringEntry      ; 6-arg call

breakpoints.why: Entry point of the vulnerable parsing function. At entry: r9w (arg4) holds the externally-computed alignment offset, r11 will be loaded with arg6 (start pointer at [rsp+0x30]), r10d will be loaded with arg5 (remaining size at [rsp+0x28]). Check if r9w correctly matches the actual alignment of the pointer in [rsp+0x30].

The alignment computation before the first call to DfsReferralParseStringEntry. At this point: rdx = arg1+0x8c (data pointer), ecx = *(arg1+0x88) (remaining size). The lea/and/sub sequence (0x1c0002336 lea r9d,[rdx+1]; 0x1c0002343 and r9w,di; 0x1c0002347 sub r9w,dx) computes r9w (alignment). Verify r9w is 0 or 1 and matches the actual alignment of rdx.

The pool allocation: ExAllocatePoolWithTag is called with size r13d = DestinationString + DestinationString_1 + 0xbc. Check r13d against the memmove sizes that follow (0x1c0002548, 0x1c0002587) to verify the allocation is large enough.
FSCTL dispatcher. arg1 contains the IOCTL code. Set conditional breakpoint for arg1 == 0x601b0 to catch the FSCTL_DFS_GET_REFERRALS_EX path.

watch: r9w at DfsReferralParseStringEntry entry — the 2-byte alignment padding; always 0 (even pointer) or 1 (odd pointer). It cannot take any other value.
r10d after 'sub r10d, eax' at 0x1c0001e22 — this is (remaining_size - alignment). The caller already checked size >= alignment, so no underflow occurs.
r9d after 'movzx r9d, word [rax+r11]' at 0x1c0001e2f — the 16-bit length field at [alignment + start_ptr]; the following checks require it to be even and to fit in the remaining size.
r13d at the ExAllocatePoolWithTag call at 0x1c000249a — allocation size = string1_len + string2_len + 0xbc; the memmove sizes (string1_len, string2_len) are always <= this.
ecx at DfsFltHandleFsControl entry — IOCTL code, 0x601b0 for the referral path
key_offsets: 0x1c0001e22: 'sub r10d, eax' — size-alignment subtraction; if alignment (eax from r9w) is incorrect, this produces wrong remaining size
0x1c0001e2f: 'movzx r9d, word [rax+r11]' — reads length from [alignment + base]; wrong alignment reads wrong offset
0x1c0001e4d: 'cmp r10d, eax' — bounds check using potentially corrupted size; if bypassed, memcpy proceeds with bad size
0x1c0002336-0x1c000234b: 'lea r9d,[rdx+0x1]; and r9w,di; sub r9w,dx' — the 16-bit alignment computation in the caller (first parser call)
0x1c000249a: 'call ExAllocatePoolWithTag' — allocation with size from parsed referral strings
0x1c0002548 and 0x1c0002587: 'call memmove' — copies referral string data into pool allocation
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. The DFS minifilter (DfsFlt) control device must be accessible. On a system with DFS Namespace service, open a handle via CreateFile to the DFS filter device. 2. Use DeviceIoControl with dwIoControlCode=0x601b0 (FSCTL_DFS_GET_REFERRALS_EX, also known as FSCTL_DFS_GET_REFERRALS_EX). 3. The input buffer (lpInBuffer) must be at least 0x88 bytes and contain a valid DFS referral request header. Key fields: at byte 0x00: version (2 or 0x17 for v2/v4 referral). At byte 0x80: a 32-bit size field (offset to referral entry, must be >= 9). At byte 0x88: second 32-bit size field (remaining data size). At byte 0x8c: referral string data begins. 4. The string length fields are validated (must be even and fit within the remaining size); the pool allocation is sized to the validated lengths and the copies always fit, so no crafted input overflows on this path. 5. Note: ExGetPreviousMode() must return 0 (KernelMode) or the function returns early — this means the attack vector is via the minifilter's internal FSCTL dispatch path (IRP_MJ_FILE_SYSTEM_CONTROL), reachable through DFS server or other kernel-mode DFS consumers.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: No crash. The alignment value is always 0 or 1, the parser rejects malformed lengths, and the pool allocation is always large enough for the two memmove copies. The request completes with STATUS_SUCCESS or a parse error (e.g. 0xC000000D). This behavior is identical in the unpatched and patched builds.
debugging_aids.notes: The unpatched build splits the alignment computation between caller and callee; the patched build performs it inside DfsReferralParseStringEntry. Both use 16-bit register operations and produce the same 0/1 padding, and both run the identical bounds checks -- this is an interface refactoring, not a truncation fix. The ExGetPreviousMode() check at entry means the path is reached from kernel context. The FSCTL 0x601b0 dispatch is in DfsFltHandleFsControl at 0x1c0009d7c. Key input buffer offsets: 0x00=version, 0x80=first_size, 0x88=second_size, 0x8c=string_data_start, 0x86=flags (bit 0 controls second string parsing).

summary: This patch contains no security-relevant fix. The most-changed function, DfsReferralParseStringEntry (sub_1C0001E04 / sub_140002210), was refactored from a 6-argument to a 3-argument interface: the unpatched build computes the 2-byte alignment padding in the caller DfsFltFsctlGetReferralEx and passes it in; the patched build computes the identical value inside the callee. The alignment padding is 0 or 1 (it depends only on the pointer's low bit) and is computed with 16-bit register operations in BOTH builds, so there is no truncation and no bounds bypass; both builds run the same four length/size checks and the caller's pool allocation and memmove copies use the same validated lengths. The originally reported out-of-bounds read / pool overflow does not exist in either build. The DfsFltFormPrefix '*arg1 <= 0' empty-prefix guard is present in BOTH builds (not added by the patch). The remaining changes across the binary are modernizations: ExAllocatePoolWithTag -> ExAllocatePool2 migration (with zero-initialization), CFG-protected indirect calls (_guard_dispatch_icall), stack zero-initialization (memset), and WPP/ETW tracing refactoring.
